input.background: A randomized placebo-controlled trial was carried out to investigate the capacity of aspirin to prevent pregnancy-induced hypertension and preeclamptic toxaemia (Schiff et al., 1989). The study was conducted on women at high risk of these conditions.
input.question: The observed rates of hypertension were 11/31 (36%) in the placebo group and 4/34 (12%) in the aspirin group; the authors quoted P=0.024 for this difference. They also quoted a relative risk (RR) of hypertension of 0.33 among the aspirin group. Calculate a 95% confidence interval for the RR, and comment on the authors' conclusion that large scale clinical trials are needed.

output.answer: Provide the calculation for the 95% confidence interval for the relative risk (RR) and discuss the implications of the authors' conclusion regarding the need for large-scale clinical trials.
